subject: mathematics
title: Grade 5 Mathematics
description: Order of operations, volume, coordinate planes, and fraction operations.

lessons.title: Order of Operations
lessons.objective: Learn BIDMAS/BODMAS rules for solving expressions.
lessons.content:
When an expression has multiple operations, use BIDMAS:

  B — Brackets first
  I — Indices (powers)
  D — Division
  M — Multiplication
  A — Addition
  S — Subtraction

Division and Multiplication are done left to right.
Addition and Subtraction are done left to right.

Example: 3 + 4 × 2 = 3 + 8 = 11 (NOT 14!)
Example: (3 + 4) × 2 = 7 × 2 = 14 (brackets change order!)

exercises.question: What is 2 + 3 × 4?
exercises.hint: Remember BIDMAS — multiplication comes before addition.
exercises.explanation: First do 3 × 4 = 12, then 2 + 12 = 14. Multiplication before addition!

exercises.question: What is 20 ÷ 4 + 3 × 2?
exercises.hint: Do division and multiplication first (left to right), then addition.

lessons.title: Volume of 3D Shapes
lessons.objective: Calculate the volume of rectangular prisms (cuboids).
lessons.content:
Volume measures the space inside a 3D shape.

For a rectangular prism (cuboid):
  Volume = length × width × height

Volume is measured in cubic units: cm³, m³

Example: A box is 5 cm long, 3 cm wide, and 2 cm tall.
  Volume = 5 × 3 × 2 = 30 cm³

A cube has all sides equal, so:
  Volume of cube = side × side × side = side³

exercises.question: What is the volume of a box that is 4 cm × 3 cm × 2 cm?
exercises.explanation: Volume = 4 × 3 × 2 = 24 cm³. We multiply all three dimensions!

exercises.question: A cube has sides of 5 cm. What is its volume in cm³? (number only)
exercises.hint: For a cube, volume = side × side × side
